# Slice 4.6 preparation — minix.rs codebase report

All paths are absolute. The authoritative spec for 4.6 itself is `docs/plan.md:997-1007` (fork = `SYS_FORK`+`VM_FORK`+`SCHEDULING_START`; exit = zombie+SIGCHLD; wait/waitpid = reap; forked children share `USER_PRIV_ID` via `PRIVCTL_SET_USER`; `VM_FORK` clones `ClientRegions`).

## 1. PM server (`servers/pm/`)

**`servers/pm/src/main.rs`** — receive loop and dispatch:
- Main loop `servers/pm/src/main.rs:90-105`. `match msg.m_type`: `NOTIFY_MESSAGE if msg.m_source == system => drain_ksigs` (`:99`), `PM_GETPID => handle_getpid` (`:100`), else drop (`:102`). Uses `sef.receive(&mut msg)` from server-rt.
- `pm_init` `:110-120`: `sef_publish_to_ds(name)` → `mproc::seed()` → `privctl_set_user(SYSTEM, STUB_E)` (releasing frozen stub E — the explicit 4.6-fork stand-in, `:116-119`).
- `handle_getpid` `:127-142`: identifies caller by kernel-stamped `msg.m_source` (no spoofable payload), looks up `mproc::getpid`, writes ppid into payload `0..4` via `wr_i32`, sets `msg.m_type = pid` (or `ESRCH`), `msg.m_source = 0`, then **replies via `ipc_send(caller_e, msg)`** — this is the SENDREC-reply pattern (`:139-141`).
- `drain_ksigs` `:154-182`: loops `SYS_GETKSIG` (SENDREC to SYSTEM), reads target endpoint from reply payload `0..4`; on `NONE` returns; disposition via `mproc::handle_kill` → if `Terminate`, `sys_exit` **before** `sys_endksig` (order matters, `:177-180`).
- Kernel-call helpers, all `ipc_sendrec(system, &mut m)` with `wr_i32` payloads: `sys_exit` (target in `0..4`, `:186-194`), `sys_endksig` (`:198-206`), `privctl_set_user` (target `0..4`, `PRIVCTL_SET_USER` in `4..8`, returns `m.m_type`, `:211-224`).
- Payload accessors are **local** `rd_i32`/`wr_i32` (native-endian, `:228-236`). PM currently only reads/writes i32; no u64 helper here.

**`servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`** — the table:
- `MProc { pid: i32, parent_slot: usize, flags: u8 }` `:46-51`; `EMPTY` `:54-58`.
- Flags: `MF_IN_USE=1<<0` (`:36`), `MF_PRIV_PROC=1<<1` (kill path refuses, `:38`), `MF_DEAD=1<<2` (kill marks dead, slot **kept, not reusable** until 4.6 zombie/reap, `:39-41`). **No `MF_ZOMBIE` / exit-status field yet.**
- `NR_MPROCS=32` `:33` — slots 0..16 seeded, 16..32 is headroom explicitly reserved for fork (`:31-32`).
- `seed_in` `:74-119`: PM=pid 0 self-parent; INIT=pid 1; boot servers next pids parented to RS (RS→PM); stubs parented to INIT. **Pid allocation is a one-shot `next_pid` counter at seed time only** (`:96,107,117`) — there is no running pid allocator or free-slot finder for fork.
- `getpid_in` `:123-129` (returns `(pid, ppid)`, `None` if not in-use or dead); `handle_kill_in` `:133-145` (returns `KillAction`, marks `MF_DEAD`); `KillAction { Terminate, SkipPrivileged, NotFound }` `:62-70`.
- Static `Table(UnsafeCell<[MProc; NR_MPROCS]>)` + `unsafe impl Sync`, single-mutator invariant `:149-157`; public wrappers `seed`/`getpid`/`handle_kill` `:160-180`. Host tests `:182-284`.

**What 4.6 needs to add to PM:** `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` dispatch arms (`main.rs:94-103`); a real pid allocator + free-`MF_IN_USE`-slot finder in `mproc.rs`; child endpoint storage (comment at `mproc.rs:45` notes fork adds generations and will store endpoints — currently endpoint is derived as `boot_endpoint(slot)`); zombie flag + exit-status + reap logic; SIGCHLD-to-parent (send `SEF_SIGNAL` to parent, see §5); **reply-to-two-callers after fork** — the existing `handle_getpid` reply pattern (`ipc_send` with `m_source=0`) applies twice: parent gets child pid in `m_type`, child gets 0 in `m_type`. PM must also learn SCHED's endpoint to drive `SCHEDULING_START` (see §4).

## 2. kernel-shared constants

**`kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`:**
- **PM block:** `PM_RQ_BASE=0x700` (`:160`), `PM_GETPID=PM_RQ_BASE` (`:167`), `NR_PM_MSGS=1` (`:171`). Const-asserts `:175-177` (above kernel-call range, below `VM_RQ_BASE`, below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`). → `PM_FORK=0x701`, `PM_EXIT=0x702`, `PM_WAIT=0x703`; bump `NR_PM_MSGS` to 4. Test patterns to extend: `pm_msgs_contiguous_from_base` (`:576`), `pm_msgs_distinct_from_other_ranges` (`:587`).
- **VM block:** `VM_RQ_BASE=0xC00` (`:190`); `VM_PAGEFAULT`=base, `VM_BRK=+1`, `VM_MMAP=+2`, `VM_MUNMAP=+3` (`:196-221`). → **`VM_FORK` = `VM_RQ_BASE+4` (0xC04)**. **Gotcha:** the VM range has **no `NR_VM_*` count const** (unlike PM/DS/SCHED/SEF), and the SEF-vs-VM ordering const-assert hardcodes `VM_RQ_BASE + 3` at `callnr.rs:311` — adding `VM_FORK` at +4 requires updating that assert (`SEF_RQ_BASE > VM_RQ_BASE + 4`). Test to extend: the `vm_*_follows_*` chain (`:437-469`).
- **SCHED block:** `SCHED_RQ_BASE=0xF00` (`:331`); `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`=base, `SCHEDULING_START=+1`, `SCHEDULING_STOP=+2`, `SCHEDULING_SET_NICE=+3`; `NR_SCHED_MSGS=4` (`:355`). `SCHEDULING_START` payload documented `:338-342`: **endpoint `0..4`, priority `4..8`, quantum(ms) `8..12`, all i32**.
- **SYS_\* kernel calls:** `KERNEL_CALL=0x600` (`:11`); `SYS_FORK=+2` (`:16`), `SYS_EXIT=+4` (`:18`) — **both numbers already exist**, no new SYS_* needed. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4=18` (`:60`), `NR_SYS_CALLS=32` (`:65`); const-asserts `NR_SYS_CALLS >= NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` and `is_multiple_of(32)` (`:67-68`). The kernel dispatcher also const-asserts `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18` at `kernel/src/system/mod.rs:200`. **`SYS_FORK` is still an ENOSYS stub** (`kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:25`); `SYS_EXIT` is real (`do_exit.rs`, but "exit-lite" — see §below). So 4.6 replaces `do_fork` and completes `do_exit`.

**`kernel-shared/src/signal.rs`:** `SIGKILL=9`, `SIGSEGV=11`, `SIGTERM=15`, `NSIG=32` (`:11-20`). **`SIGCHLD` is NOT present** — must be added (POSIX/MINIX value 17) with a `const _: () = assert!(SIGCHLD > 0 && (SIGCHLD as usize) < NSIG)` and a line in `signal_numbers_match_posix` (`:31`).

**`kernel-shared/src/com.rs`:** `STUB_A..E_PROC_NR = 11..15` (`:80-90`), `NR_STUB_PROCS=5` (`:93`); server procs `PM=0`…`SCHED=9`,`INIT=10` (`:48-58`); `NR_BOOT_PROCS=11` (`:62`); `boot_endpoint(p)` (`:101-103`). `NR_PROCS=1024` (`:16`), `NR_SYS_PROCS=64` (`:18`).

**`kernel-shared/src/error.rs`:** all four present — `ESRCH=-4` (`:13`), `ECHILD=-11` (`:20`), `EAGAIN=-12` (`:21`), `EDEADSRCDST=-105` (`:37`).

**Const-assert conventions:** each server request range is asserted strictly above the previous range's top and strictly below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE=0x1000`; each range carries an `NR_*` count const that "locks dispatch coverage"; host tests follow `*_contiguous_from_base` + `*_distinct_from_other_ranges` naming (`callnr.rs:364-632`).

## 3. VM server (`servers/vm/`)

**`servers/vm/src/region.rs`:**
- `Kind { Unused, Heap, Mmap }` (`:56-60`); `Region { start, end, kind }` + `contains` (`:64-80`).
- `ClientRegions { regions: [Region; MAX_REGIONS], mmap_next: u64 }` (`:84-90`), `EMPTY` sets `mmap_next = MMAP_BASE` (`:93-96`). `MAX_REGIONS=4` (`:52`), `MAX_CLIENTS=16` (`:48`), `HEAP_BASE=0x0100_0000` (`:32`), `MMAP_BASE=0x0200_0000` (`:42`).
- Signatures: `set_brk(&mut self, new_break) -> Result<u64,i32>` (`:107`), `contains(&self, addr) -> bool` (`:99`), `mmap(&mut self, len) -> Result<u64,i32>` (`:143`), `munmap(&mut self, addr, len) -> Result<(u64,u64),i32>` (`:179`).
- Static `RegionTable(UnsafeCell<[ClientRegions; MAX_CLIENTS]>)` **keyed by proc number** (`:203-210`); `client_idx(nr)` maps proc-nr→index, rejects negative and `≥ MAX_CLIENTS` (`:214-217`); public `contains`/`set_brk`/`mmap`/`munmap` take `nr: i32` (`:238-268`). Host tests `:270-465`.
- **Proc-slot-reuse hazard is explicitly documented** at `region.rs:22-23`: "Phase 4's fork will churn proc numbers past this cap and revisit the keying." There is **no clear-slot API** today — `VM_FORK` must add one (clear child `ClientRegions` before copy) or the child would inherit a stale predecessor's regions.

**`servers/vm/src/main.rs`:**
- Dispatch `:107-115`: `VM_PAGEFAULT`/`VM_BRK`/`VM_MMAP`/`VM_MUNMAP`. **Client proc is derived from the message endpoint, not carried in payload:** `endpoint_proc(caller_e).get()` where `caller_e = msg.m_source` (e.g. `:184`, `:207`, `:233`; page-fault uses `msg.m_source` at `:108,134-135`). So the region table is keyed by **proc number decoded from `m_source`**.
- SYS_VMCTL marshaling (payload layout reference): `VMCTL_PT_MAP` subcall `0..4`, target `4..8`, vaddr(u64) `8..16`, prot `16..20` (`:160-163`); `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP` `:246-248`. `SYS_KILL` marshaling: target `0..4`, `SIGSEGV` `4..8` (`:144-145`). Local accessors `rd_u64`/`wr_i32`/`wr_u64` (`:264-276`).

**What `VM_FORK(parent, child)` needs:** a new dispatch arm + handler that reads two proc numbers (parent, child) **from the payload** (unlike other VM requests which use `m_source`, since PM is the caller here), clears the child slot, then copies `parent.regions` + `parent.mmap_next`. Requires a new `region::` public fn (e.g. `fork(parent_nr, child_nr)`). Note `MAX_CLIENTS=16` means forked children (proc nr ≥ 16) fall outside the current table — the keying cap must be revisited per the module note.

## 4. SCHED server (`servers/sched/`)

**`servers/sched/src/policy.rs`:** `SchedProc { proc_nr, priority, quantum, in_use }` (`:45-50`), `CAP=16` (`:41`), `USER_Q=8` (`:32`), `QUANTUM=5` (`:36`). Pure helpers `schedule_in`/`record_in`/`renice_in`/`forget_in`; public `schedule_proc`/`record`/`renice`/`forget` keyed by `proc_nr` (`:164-194`). Host tests `:196-298`.

**`servers/sched/src/main.rs`:** **`SCHEDULING_START` is already fully implemented.** `handle_start` `:129-140` reads `target_e` (`0..4`), `priority` (`4..8`), `quantum` (`8..12`), then `sys_schedctl(system, target_e, 0)` (claim), `policy::record`, `sys_schedule`, `reply(caller_e, OK)`. `handle_stop` `:145-153`, `handle_set_nice` `:159-172`. Kernel-call glue: `sys_schedule` (target `0..4`, prio `4..8`, quantum `8..12`, `:179-189`), `sys_schedctl` (flags `0..4`, target `4..8`, `:194-203`).

**`SYS_SCHEDCTL` claim** (`kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs:140-171`): `flags=0` → `target.scheduler = caller` (SCHED's endpoint); `SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `scheduler = NONE`. `SYS_SCHEDULE` (`:71-131`) sets priority+quantum and (re-)admits to the run queue.

**Who calls START today:** **nobody.** The kernel pre-delegates stub C directly (`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs:238-249` sets `p.scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)`), which the comment explicitly calls a stand-in "for the `SCHEDULING_START` a real RS/PM will issue" in 4.5/4.6. **So the SCHED side needs no changes for 4.6** — the work is entirely on the PM side: PM must SENDREC `SCHEDULING_START` to SCHED for each forked child. PM needs SCHED's endpoint — either `boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)` (SCHED is a boot server, gen 0) or a `DS_RETRIEVE` lookup by name (the DS registry pattern, `server-rt/src/ds.rs`).

## 5. server-rt (`server-rt/src/`)

- **`sef.rs`:** `SefConfig { init_fresh, signal_handler }` (`:18-23`); `sef_startup` does the `SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI)` handshake and runs init (`:40-81`); `Sef::receive` (`:99-138`) loops `ipc_receive(ANY, msg)`, `classify`, handles `Ping` (notify RS back), `Signal(signo)` (invokes registered handler, `:120-124`), `Init` (swallowed), returns only `Application`.
- **`classify.rs`:** `classify` (`:50-66`) gates every control event on `m_source`: `NOTIFY_MESSAGE from RS → Ping`; `SEF_SIGNAL from PM or RS → Signal(signo)` (signo decoded from payload `0..4`, `:55-62`); `SEF_INIT from RS → Init`; **`NOTIFY from SYSTEM → Application`** (so PM's ksig wake reaches PM's own dispatch — tested at `:102-110`). This is the mechanism for **SIGCHLD-to-parent**: PM sends `SEF_SIGNAL` (m_type=`SEF_SIGNAL`, signo in payload `0..4`) to the parent; server-rt classifies it as `Signal` since it's from PM.
- **`lib.rs` exports** (`:40-44`): `SefEvent`/`classify`, `sef_publish_to_ds`, `SefInitCb`, `Sef`/`SefConfig`/`sef_startup`, `SefSignalCb`. Crate is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (`:32`).
- **`minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` API surface:** `ipc_send(dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:28`), `ipc_receive(src, &mut Message)->i32` (`:35`), `ipc_sendrec(src_dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:42`), `ipc_sendnb(dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:49`), `ipc_notify(dest)->i32` (no message buffer, `:58`). aarch64 SVC trap; off-target fallbacks are `unreachable!` (`:115-126`).
- **`sef_publish_to_ds`** (`ds.rs:35-48`): builds key-only `DS_PUBLISH` (name in `0..SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN`), SENDRECs to DS, returns `m_type`. `DS_RETRIEVE` reply carries endpoint in payload `16..20` (`callnr.rs:255-258`) — the pattern PM would use to look up SCHED/VM.
- **No generic `SYS_*` helper exists** — every server hand-marshals kernel calls with local `rd/wr` helpers. Reference payload layouts: `SYS_KILL` target `0..4` + sig `4..8` (`kernel/src/system/do_sig.rs:29-38, 76-77`); `SYS_VMCTL` subcall/target/vaddr/prot (`vm/main.rs:160-163`); `SYS_EXIT` target `0..4` (`do_exit.rs:27-31`); `SYS_PRIVCTL` target `0..4` + subcode `4..8` (`do_privctl.rs:21-26`); `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_SCHEDCTL` (`do_schedule.rs:27-35`).

## 6. Message payload conventions

- **`kernel-shared/src/message.rs`:** `Message { m_source: Endpoint (i32), m_type: i32, payload: [u8; 96] }`, `#[repr(C, align(8))]`, 104 bytes (`:33-46`). **There are NO `payload_u32`/`payload_u64` accessor methods** on `Message`. Every caller defines local `rd_i32`/`wr_i32`/`rd_u64`/`wr_u64` helpers using `i32::from_ne_bytes`/`to_ne_bytes` etc. — **native-endian**, offset-indexed byte slices. Instances: `pm/main.rs:228-236`, `vm/main.rs:264-276`, `sched/main.rs:215-227`, `kernel/system/do_sig.rs:221-238`, `do_exit.rs:141-148`, `do_privctl.rs:102-109`, `do_schedule.rs:173-180`.
- Concrete layouts referenced by the task: PM_GETPID ppid in payload `0..4` i32 (`pm/main.rs:133`, `callnr.rs:167`); VM_BRK addr `0..8` u64 (`vm/main.rs:185`, `callnr.rs:203`); SYS_KILL target `0..4` + sig `4..8` i32 (`do_sig.rs:76-77`). Any 4.6 PM_FORK/PM_WAIT payload (e.g. child pid, exit status, waited pid) should follow the same native-endian i32/u64 offset convention.

## 7. Coverage / test conventions

- **`sonar-project.properties`** `sonar.coverage.exclusions` = `servers/**/src/main.rs, drivers/**/src/main.rs, minix-ipc/src/**, server-rt/src/sef.rs, server-rt/src/ds.rs`. **Implication:** any new PM fork/exit/wait *logic* that should count toward coverage must live in a host-testable submodule (like `mproc.rs`), not in `main.rs`. Same for VM_FORK region cloning (put it in `region.rs`) and any SCHED changes (`policy.rs`).
- **Crates with host tests** (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`): `kernel-shared` (all modules: callnr/com/endpoint/error/ipc_const/message/signal), `server-rt/src/classify.rs`, `servers/vm/src/region.rs`, `servers/ds/src/registry.rs`, `servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`, `servers/sched/src/policy.rs`, `servers/rs/src/monitor.rs`, `kernel/src/ipc/message.rs`.
- **miri CI** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, miri job): `cargo miri test -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-vm -p minixrs-pm` — slice 4.5 added `-p minixrs-pm`. **`minixrs-sched` is NOT in the miri list** — if 4.6 adds unsafe-touching logic to SCHED it wouldn't be miri-covered (though SCHED likely needs no change). `minixrs-vm` is already covered, so `VM_FORK` region logic gets miri automatically. Package names: `minixrs-pm`, `minixrs-vm`, `minixrs-sched`, `minixrs-kernel-shared`, `minixrs-server-rt`, `minixrs-ds`, `minixrs-rs`, `minixrs-ipc`.

## Kernel-side context 4.6 must complete (not user-space, but load-bearing)

- **`SYS_FORK` is an ENOSYS stub** (`kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:25`); dispatched caller-locally at `kernel/src/system/mod.rs:206`. 4.6 replaces it (free slot, copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy, **must zero `sig_pending` on slot reuse** — noted at `kernel/src/proc/proc_struct.rs:63-64`).
- **`SYS_EXIT` is "exit-lite"** (`kernel/src/system/do_exit.rs`): today only `RTS_PROC_STOP` + `alarm_at=0` + one `caller_q` unlink (`:52-102`). Deferred to 4.6 and listed at `:17-21`: AS teardown via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot (`RTS_SLOT_FREE`), generation bump, unblock receivers blocked on the dead endpoint.
- **`SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)`** (`kernel/src/system/do_privctl.rs:52-100`) is the release path for frozen children — gated on `RTS_NO_PRIV` (`:78`), assigns shared `USER_PRIV_ID` (`table.rs:342`, so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot priv table). PM already drives this for stub E and reuses it per child.
- Signal wake path PM relies on: `cause_sig` → `deliver_ksig` NOTIFY-from-SYSTEM (`kernel/src/system/do_sig.rs:115-133`); PM's SIGCHLD to a parent would instead go through the `SEF_SIGNAL` message path (§5), not the kernel ksig bitmap, unless PM raises it via `SYS_KILL`.

**Key gotchas for the implementer:** (1) `VM_RQ_BASE` has no `NR_VM_*` const and the SEF const-assert hardcodes `VM_RQ_BASE + 3` (`callnr.rs:311`) — bump it for `VM_FORK`. (2) VM/SCHED region+policy tables cap at proc-nr 16 (`MAX_CLIENTS`/`CAP` = 16) while forked children get nr ≥ 16 — the keying cap needs revisiting. (3) `SIGCHLD` is undefined in `signal.rs`. (4) PM has no pid allocator/free-slot finder yet. (5) Put testable 4.6 logic in submodules, not `main.rs`, for coverage.

content.text:
I have thorough coverage of all seven areas. Here is the structured report.

---

# Slice 4.6 preparation — minix.rs codebase report

All paths are absolute. The authoritative spec for 4.6 itself is `docs/plan.md:997-1007` (fork = `SYS_FORK`+`VM_FORK`+`SCHEDULING_START`; exit = zombie+SIGCHLD; wait/waitpid = reap; forked children share `USER_PRIV_ID` via `PRIVCTL_SET_USER`; `VM_FORK` clones `ClientRegions`).

## 1. PM server (`servers/pm/`)

**`servers/pm/src/main.rs`** — receive loop and dispatch:
- Main loop `servers/pm/src/main.rs:90-105`. `match msg.m_type`: `NOTIFY_MESSAGE if msg.m_source == system => drain_ksigs` (`:99`), `PM_GETPID => handle_getpid` (`:100`), else drop (`:102`). Uses `sef.receive(&mut msg)` from server-rt.
- `pm_init` `:110-120`: `sef_publish_to_ds(name)` → `mproc::seed()` → `privctl_set_user(SYSTEM, STUB_E)` (releasing frozen stub E — the explicit 4.6-fork stand-in, `:116-119`).
- `handle_getpid` `:127-142`: identifies caller by kernel-stamped `msg.m_source` (no spoofable payload), looks up `mproc::getpid`, writes ppid into payload `0..4` via `wr_i32`, sets `msg.m_type = pid` (or `ESRCH`), `msg.m_source = 0`, then **replies via `ipc_send(caller_e, msg)`** — this is the SENDREC-reply pattern (`:139-141`).
- `drain_ksigs` `:154-182`: loops `SYS_GETKSIG` (SENDREC to SYSTEM), reads target endpoint from reply payload `0..4`; on `NONE` returns; disposition via `mproc::handle_kill` → if `Terminate`, `sys_exit` **before** `sys_endksig` (order matters, `:177-180`).
- Kernel-call helpers, all `ipc_sendrec(system, &mut m)` with `wr_i32` payloads: `sys_exit` (target in `0..4`, `:186-194`), `sys_endksig` (`:198-206`), `privctl_set_user` (target `0..4`, `PRIVCTL_SET_USER` in `4..8`, returns `m.m_type`, `:211-224`).
- Payload accessors are **local** `rd_i32`/`wr_i32` (native-endian, `:228-236`). PM currently only reads/writes i32; no u64 helper here.

**`servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`** — the table:
- `MProc { pid: i32, parent_slot: usize, flags: u8 }` `:46-51`; `EMPTY` `:54-58`.
- Flags: `MF_IN_USE=1<<0` (`:36`), `MF_PRIV_PROC=1<<1` (kill path refuses, `:38`), `MF_DEAD=1<<2` (kill marks dead, slot **kept, not reusable** until 4.6 zombie/reap, `:39-41`). **No `MF_ZOMBIE` / exit-status field yet.**
- `NR_MPROCS=32` `:33` — slots 0..16 seeded, 16..32 is headroom explicitly reserved for fork (`:31-32`).
- `seed_in` `:74-119`: PM=pid 0 self-parent; INIT=pid 1; boot servers next pids parented to RS (RS→PM); stubs parented to INIT. **Pid allocation is a one-shot `next_pid` counter at seed time only** (`:96,107,117`) — there is no running pid allocator or free-slot finder for fork.
- `getpid_in` `:123-129` (returns `(pid, ppid)`, `None` if not in-use or dead); `handle_kill_in` `:133-145` (returns `KillAction`, marks `MF_DEAD`); `KillAction { Terminate, SkipPrivileged, NotFound }` `:62-70`.
- Static `Table(UnsafeCell<[MProc; NR_MPROCS]>)` + `unsafe impl Sync`, single-mutator invariant `:149-157`; public wrappers `seed`/`getpid`/`handle_kill` `:160-180`. Host tests `:182-284`.

**What 4.6 needs to add to PM:** `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` dispatch arms (`main.rs:94-103`); a real pid allocator + free-`MF_IN_USE`-slot finder in `mproc.rs`; child endpoint storage (comment at `mproc.rs:45` notes fork adds generations and will store endpoints — currently endpoint is derived as `boot_endpoint(slot)`); zombie flag + exit-status + reap logic; SIGCHLD-to-parent (send `SEF_SIGNAL` to parent, see §5); **reply-to-two-callers after fork** — the existing `handle_getpid` reply pattern (`ipc_send` with `m_source=0`) applies twice: parent gets child pid in `m_type`, child gets 0 in `m_type`. PM must also learn SCHED's endpoint to drive `SCHEDULING_START` (see §4).

## 2. kernel-shared constants

**`kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`:**
- **PM block:** `PM_RQ_BASE=0x700` (`:160`), `PM_GETPID=PM_RQ_BASE` (`:167`), `NR_PM_MSGS=1` (`:171`). Const-asserts `:175-177` (above kernel-call range, below `VM_RQ_BASE`, below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`). → `PM_FORK=0x701`, `PM_EXIT=0x702`, `PM_WAIT=0x703`; bump `NR_PM_MSGS` to 4. Test patterns to extend: `pm_msgs_contiguous_from_base` (`:576`), `pm_msgs_distinct_from_other_ranges` (`:587`).
- **VM block:** `VM_RQ_BASE=0xC00` (`:190`); `VM_PAGEFAULT`=base, `VM_BRK=+1`, `VM_MMAP=+2`, `VM_MUNMAP=+3` (`:196-221`). → **`VM_FORK` = `VM_RQ_BASE+4` (0xC04)**. **Gotcha:** the VM range has **no `NR_VM_*` count const** (unlike PM/DS/SCHED/SEF), and the SEF-vs-VM ordering const-assert hardcodes `VM_RQ_BASE + 3` at `callnr.rs:311` — adding `VM_FORK` at +4 requires updating that assert (`SEF_RQ_BASE > VM_RQ_BASE + 4`). Test to extend: the `vm_*_follows_*` chain (`:437-469`).
- **SCHED block:** `SCHED_RQ_BASE=0xF00` (`:331`); `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`=base, `SCHEDULING_START=+1`, `SCHEDULING_STOP=+2`, `SCHEDULING_SET_NICE=+3`; `NR_SCHED_MSGS=4` (`:355`). `SCHEDULING_START` payload documented `:338-342`: **endpoint `0..4`, priority `4..8`, quantum(ms) `8..12`, all i32**.
- **SYS_\* kernel calls:** `KERNEL_CALL=0x600` (`:11`); `SYS_FORK=+2` (`:16`), `SYS_EXIT=+4` (`:18`) — **both numbers already exist**, no new SYS_* needed. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4=18` (`:60`), `NR_SYS_CALLS=32` (`:65`); const-asserts `NR_SYS_CALLS >= NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` and `is_multiple_of(32)` (`:67-68`). The kernel dispatcher also const-asserts `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18` at `kernel/src/system/mod.rs:200`. **`SYS_FORK` is still an ENOSYS stub** (`kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:25`); `SYS_EXIT` is real (`do_exit.rs`, but "exit-lite" — see §below). So 4.6 replaces `do_fork` and completes `do_exit`.

**`kernel-shared/src/signal.rs`:** `SIGKILL=9`, `SIGSEGV=11`, `SIGTERM=15`, `NSIG=32` (`:11-20`). **`SIGCHLD` is NOT present** — must be added (POSIX/MINIX value 17) with a `const _: () = assert!(SIGCHLD > 0 && (SIGCHLD as usize) < NSIG)` and a line in `signal_numbers_match_posix` (`:31`).

**`kernel-shared/src/com.rs`:** `STUB_A..E_PROC_NR = 11..15` (`:80-90`), `NR_STUB_PROCS=5` (`:93`); server procs `PM=0`…`SCHED=9`,`INIT=10` (`:48-58`); `NR_BOOT_PROCS=11` (`:62`); `boot_endpoint(p)` (`:101-103`). `NR_PROCS=1024` (`:16`), `NR_SYS_PROCS=64` (`:18`).

**`kernel-shared/src/error.rs`:** all four present — `ESRCH=-4` (`:13`), `ECHILD=-11` (`:20`), `EAGAIN=-12` (`:21`), `EDEADSRCDST=-105` (`:37`).

**Const-assert conventions:** each server request range is asserted strictly above the previous range's top and strictly below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE=0x1000`; each range carries an `NR_*` count const that "locks dispatch coverage"; host tests follow `*_contiguous_from_base` + `*_distinct_from_other_ranges` naming (`callnr.rs:364-632`).

## 3. VM server (`servers/vm/`)

**`servers/vm/src/region.rs`:**
- `Kind { Unused, Heap, Mmap }` (`:56-60`); `Region { start, end, kind }` + `contains` (`:64-80`).
- `ClientRegions { regions: [Region; MAX_REGIONS], mmap_next: u64 }` (`:84-90`), `EMPTY` sets `mmap_next = MMAP_BASE` (`:93-96`). `MAX_REGIONS=4` (`:52`), `MAX_CLIENTS=16` (`:48`), `HEAP_BASE=0x0100_0000` (`:32`), `MMAP_BASE=0x0200_0000` (`:42`).
- Signatures: `set_brk(&mut self, new_break) -> Result<u64,i32>` (`:107`), `contains(&self, addr) -> bool` (`:99`), `mmap(&mut self, len) -> Result<u64,i32>` (`:143`), `munmap(&mut self, addr, len) -> Result<(u64,u64),i32>` (`:179`).
- Static `RegionTable(UnsafeCell<[ClientRegions; MAX_CLIENTS]>)` **keyed by proc number** (`:203-210`); `client_idx(nr)` maps proc-nr→index, rejects negative and `≥ MAX_CLIENTS` (`:214-217`); public `contains`/`set_brk`/`mmap`/`munmap` take `nr: i32` (`:238-268`). Host tests `:270-465`.
- **Proc-slot-reuse hazard is explicitly documented** at `region.rs:22-23`: "Phase 4's fork will churn proc numbers past this cap and revisit the keying." There is **no clear-slot API** today — `VM_FORK` must add one (clear child `ClientRegions` before copy) or the child would inherit a stale predecessor's regions.

**`servers/vm/src/main.rs`:**
- Dispatch `:107-115`: `VM_PAGEFAULT`/`VM_BRK`/`VM_MMAP`/`VM_MUNMAP`. **Client proc is derived from the message endpoint, not carried in payload:** `endpoint_proc(caller_e).get()` where `caller_e = msg.m_source` (e.g. `:184`, `:207`, `:233`; page-fault uses `msg.m_source` at `:108,134-135`). So the region table is keyed by **proc number decoded from `m_source`**.
- SYS_VMCTL marshaling (payload layout reference): `VMCTL_PT_MAP` subcall `0..4`, target `4..8`, vaddr(u64) `8..16`, prot `16..20` (`:160-163`); `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP` `:246-248`. `SYS_KILL` marshaling: target `0..4`, `SIGSEGV` `4..8` (`:144-145`). Local accessors `rd_u64`/`wr_i32`/`wr_u64` (`:264-276`).

**What `VM_FORK(parent, child)` needs:** a new dispatch arm + handler that reads two proc numbers (parent, child) **from the payload** (unlike other VM requests which use `m_source`, since PM is the caller here), clears the child slot, then copies `parent.regions` + `parent.mmap_next`. Requires a new `region::` public fn (e.g. `fork(parent_nr, child_nr)`). Note `MAX_CLIENTS=16` means forked children (proc nr ≥ 16) fall outside the current table — the keying cap must be revisited per the module note.

## 4. SCHED server (`servers/sched/`)

**`servers/sched/src/policy.rs`:** `SchedProc { proc_nr, priority, quantum, in_use }` (`:45-50`), `CAP=16` (`:41`), `USER_Q=8` (`:32`), `QUANTUM=5` (`:36`). Pure helpers `schedule_in`/`record_in`/`renice_in`/`forget_in`; public `schedule_proc`/`record`/`renice`/`forget` keyed by `proc_nr` (`:164-194`). Host tests `:196-298`.

**`servers/sched/src/main.rs`:** **`SCHEDULING_START` is already fully implemented.** `handle_start` `:129-140` reads `target_e` (`0..4`), `priority` (`4..8`), `quantum` (`8..12`), then `sys_schedctl(system, target_e, 0)` (claim), `policy::record`, `sys_schedule`, `reply(caller_e, OK)`. `handle_stop` `:145-153`, `handle_set_nice` `:159-172`. Kernel-call glue: `sys_schedule` (target `0..4`, prio `4..8`, quantum `8..12`, `:179-189`), `sys_schedctl` (flags `0..4`, target `4..8`, `:194-203`).

**`SYS_SCHEDCTL` claim** (`kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs:140-171`): `flags=0` → `target.scheduler = caller` (SCHED's endpoint); `SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `scheduler = NONE`. `SYS_SCHEDULE` (`:71-131`) sets priority+quantum and (re-)admits to the run queue.

**Who calls START today:** **nobody.** The kernel pre-delegates stub C directly (`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs:238-249` sets `p.scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)`), which the comment explicitly calls a stand-in "for the `SCHEDULING_START` a real RS/PM will issue" in 4.5/4.6. **So the SCHED side needs no changes for 4.6** — the work is entirely on the PM side: PM must SENDREC `SCHEDULING_START` to SCHED for each forked child. PM needs SCHED's endpoint — either `boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)` (SCHED is a boot server, gen 0) or a `DS_RETRIEVE` lookup by name (the DS registry pattern, `server-rt/src/ds.rs`).

## 5. server-rt (`server-rt/src/`)

- **`sef.rs`:** `SefConfig { init_fresh, signal_handler }` (`:18-23`); `sef_startup` does the `SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI)` handshake and runs init (`:40-81`); `Sef::receive` (`:99-138`) loops `ipc_receive(ANY, msg)`, `classify`, handles `Ping` (notify RS back), `Signal(signo)` (invokes registered handler, `:120-124`), `Init` (swallowed), returns only `Application`.
- **`classify.rs`:** `classify` (`:50-66`) gates every control event on `m_source`: `NOTIFY_MESSAGE from RS → Ping`; `SEF_SIGNAL from PM or RS → Signal(signo)` (signo decoded from payload `0..4`, `:55-62`); `SEF_INIT from RS → Init`; **`NOTIFY from SYSTEM → Application`** (so PM's ksig wake reaches PM's own dispatch — tested at `:102-110`). This is the mechanism for **SIGCHLD-to-parent**: PM sends `SEF_SIGNAL` (m_type=`SEF_SIGNAL`, signo in payload `0..4`) to the parent; server-rt classifies it as `Signal` since it's from PM.
- **`lib.rs` exports** (`:40-44`): `SefEvent`/`classify`, `sef_publish_to_ds`, `SefInitCb`, `Sef`/`SefConfig`/`sef_startup`, `SefSignalCb`. Crate is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (`:32`).
- **`minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` API surface:** `ipc_send(dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:28`), `ipc_receive(src, &mut Message)->i32` (`:35`), `ipc_sendrec(src_dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:42`), `ipc_sendnb(dest, &mut Message)->i32` (`:49`), `ipc_notify(dest)->i32` (no message buffer, `:58`). aarch64 SVC trap; off-target fallbacks are `unreachable!` (`:115-126`).
- **`sef_publish_to_ds`** (`ds.rs:35-48`): builds key-only `DS_PUBLISH` (name in `0..SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN`), SENDRECs to DS, returns `m_type`. `DS_RETRIEVE` reply carries endpoint in payload `16..20` (`callnr.rs:255-258`) — the pattern PM would use to look up SCHED/VM.
- **No generic `SYS_*` helper exists** — every server hand-marshals kernel calls with local `rd/wr` helpers. Reference payload layouts: `SYS_KILL` target `0..4` + sig `4..8` (`kernel/src/system/do_sig.rs:29-38, 76-77`); `SYS_VMCTL` subcall/target/vaddr/prot (`vm/main.rs:160-163`); `SYS_EXIT` target `0..4` (`do_exit.rs:27-31`); `SYS_PRIVCTL` target `0..4` + subcode `4..8` (`do_privctl.rs:21-26`); `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_SCHEDCTL` (`do_schedule.rs:27-35`).

## 6. Message payload conventions

- **`kernel-shared/src/message.rs`:** `Message { m_source: Endpoint (i32), m_type: i32, payload: [u8; 96] }`, `#[repr(C, align(8))]`, 104 bytes (`:33-46`). **There are NO `payload_u32`/`payload_u64` accessor methods** on `Message`. Every caller defines local `rd_i32`/`wr_i32`/`rd_u64`/`wr_u64` helpers using `i32::from_ne_bytes`/`to_ne_bytes` etc. — **native-endian**, offset-indexed byte slices. Instances: `pm/main.rs:228-236`, `vm/main.rs:264-276`, `sched/main.rs:215-227`, `kernel/system/do_sig.rs:221-238`, `do_exit.rs:141-148`, `do_privctl.rs:102-109`, `do_schedule.rs:173-180`.
- Concrete layouts referenced by the task: PM_GETPID ppid in payload `0..4` i32 (`pm/main.rs:133`, `callnr.rs:167`); VM_BRK addr `0..8` u64 (`vm/main.rs:185`, `callnr.rs:203`); SYS_KILL target `0..4` + sig `4..8` i32 (`do_sig.rs:76-77`). Any 4.6 PM_FORK/PM_WAIT payload (e.g. child pid, exit status, waited pid) should follow the same native-endian i32/u64 offset convention.

## 7. Coverage / test conventions

- **`sonar-project.properties`** `sonar.coverage.exclusions` = `servers/**/src/main.rs, drivers/**/src/main.rs, minix-ipc/src/**, server-rt/src/sef.rs, server-rt/src/ds.rs`. **Implication:** any new PM fork/exit/wait *logic* that should count toward coverage must live in a host-testable submodule (like `mproc.rs`), not in `main.rs`. Same for VM_FORK region cloning (put it in `region.rs`) and any SCHED changes (`policy.rs`).
- **Crates with host tests** (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`): `kernel-shared` (all modules: callnr/com/endpoint/error/ipc_const/message/signal), `server-rt/src/classify.rs`, `servers/vm/src/region.rs`, `servers/ds/src/registry.rs`, `servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`, `servers/sched/src/policy.rs`, `servers/rs/src/monitor.rs`, `kernel/src/ipc/message.rs`.
- **miri CI** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, miri job): `cargo miri test -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-vm -p minixrs-pm` — slice 4.5 added `-p minixrs-pm`. **`minixrs-sched` is NOT in the miri list** — if 4.6 adds unsafe-touching logic to SCHED it wouldn't be miri-covered (though SCHED likely needs no change). `minixrs-vm` is already covered, so `VM_FORK` region logic gets miri automatically. Package names: `minixrs-pm`, `minixrs-vm`, `minixrs-sched`, `minixrs-kernel-shared`, `minixrs-server-rt`, `minixrs-ds`, `minixrs-rs`, `minixrs-ipc`.

## Kernel-side context 4.6 must complete (not user-space, but load-bearing)

- **`SYS_FORK` is an ENOSYS stub** (`kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:25`); dispatched caller-locally at `kernel/src/system/mod.rs:206`. 4.6 replaces it (free slot, copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy, **must zero `sig_pending` on slot reuse** — noted at `kernel/src/proc/proc_struct.rs:63-64`).
- **`SYS_EXIT` is "exit-lite"** (`kernel/src/system/do_exit.rs`): today only `RTS_PROC_STOP` + `alarm_at=0` + one `caller_q` unlink (`:52-102`). Deferred to 4.6 and listed at `:17-21`: AS teardown via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot (`RTS_SLOT_FREE`), generation bump, unblock receivers blocked on the dead endpoint.
- **`SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)`** (`kernel/src/system/do_privctl.rs:52-100`) is the release path for frozen children — gated on `RTS_NO_PRIV` (`:78`), assigns shared `USER_PRIV_ID` (`table.rs:342`, so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot priv table). PM already drives this for stub E and reuses it per child.
- Signal wake path PM relies on: `cause_sig` → `deliver_ksig` NOTIFY-from-SYSTEM (`kernel/src/system/do_sig.rs:115-133`); PM's SIGCHLD to a parent would instead go through the `SEF_SIGNAL` message path (§5), not the kernel ksig bitmap, unless PM raises it via `SYS_KILL`.

**Key gotchas for the implementer:** (1) `VM_RQ_BASE` has no `NR_VM_*` const and the SEF const-assert hardcodes `VM_RQ_BASE + 3` (`callnr.rs:311`) — bump it for `VM_FORK`. (2) VM/SCHED region+policy tables cap at proc-nr 16 (`MAX_CLIENTS`/`CAP` = 16) while forked children get nr ≥ 16 — the keying cap needs revisiting. (3) `SIGCHLD` is undefined in `signal.rs`. (4) PM has no pid allocator/free-slot finder yet. (5) Put testable 4.6 logic in submodules, not `main.rs`, for coverage.

# Slice 4.6 preparation — exploration findings

## PART A — minix.rs userland glue

### A1. EL0 user stubs — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S`

**Shared conventions (all stubs):**
- Constant block (lines 40–55): `SEND=1 RECEIVE=2 SENDREC=3 ANY=0x3FFF`, endpoints `ENDPOINT_A=11 ENDPOINT_B=12 ENDPOINT_PM=0 ENDPOINT_VM=7 ENDPOINT_SYS=0x7FFE`, `PM_GETPID=0x700`, `VM_BRK=0xC01 VM_MMAP=0xC02 VM_MUNMAP=0xC03`, `MSG_SIZE=104`.
- **Message on stack:** `sub x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE` carves a 104-byte `Message` at `[SP_EL0-104, SP_EL0)`; `str wzr,[x9,#0]` zeroes `m_source` (kernel overwrites), `str w..,[x9,#4]` sets `m_type`, payload begins at `[x9,#8]`. SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 % 16 handled; stack pages are 4 KiB).
- **SVC ABI:** `x0`=src/dst endpoint, `x1`=primitive, `x2`=msg ptr, then `svc #0`. Result returns in `x0`.
- **Callee-saved counters:** `x19..x23` (AAPCS) persist across SVC because `trap.S` saves/restores `x0..x30`. This is load-bearing for stub D's `x22` (mmap base survives an intervening SVC) and every stub's `x19` counter.
- **PC-relative-free:** all blobs use only immediate `mov/movz/movk` + register-relative loads, so they run correctly copied to any code VA.

**Per-stub catalog:**
- **Stub A** (63–78) sender: `x19` counter, `SENDREC` to `ENDPOINT_B` in a loop; `m_type`=counter.
- **Stub B** (86–105) echo server: `RECEIVE(ANY)`, reads `m_source` (`sxtw` sign-extend), `SEND` back, mirrors `m_type`.
- **Stub C** (113–134) kernel-call client: `SENDREC` to `ENDPOINT_SYS` with `m_type=SYS_GETINFO`, `payload[0..4]=GET_WHOAMI`.
- **Stub D** (176–263) VM driver: `VM_BRK`→touch→`VM_BRK`→touch→`VM_MMAP`(base returned in `x22`)→touch→`VM_MUNMAP`→32-iter heap steady phase→**deliberate out-of-region store to `x22`** to trigger SIGSEGV (the slice-4.5 kill chain).
- **Stub E** (281–299) — **the 4.6 template**, PM getpid client. Exact sequence:
  ```
  mov  x19, #0                 // counter
  1:  sub  x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE   // 104-byte msg buffer
      str  wzr, [x9, #0]       // m_source = 0 (kernel overwrites)
      mov  w10, #PM_GETPID     // 0x700
      str  w10, [x9, #4]       // m_type; no request payload
      mov  x0, #ENDPOINT_PM    // 0
      mov  x1, #SENDREC        // 3
      mov  x2, x9
      svc  #0
      add  x19, x19, #1
      b    1b
  ```
  E never reads the reply body (reply `m_type`=E's pid, `payload[0..4]`=parent pid). **For 4.6**: a fork/wait/exit exerciser would follow this exact PM-SENDREC shape but with new `m_type` values `PM_RQ_BASE(0x700)+n`. Today only `PM_GETPID` exists (see A-notes on `callnr.rs`); `NR_PM_MSGS` locks the PM message count and would need widening.

### A2. Bootstrap wiring — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`

- **`build_stub`** signature (359–369): `(nr, priv_id: Option<PrivId>, id, stub_start, stub_end, code_va, stack_va, heap_window, frozen: bool)`. Allocates a fresh `AddrSpace`, copies the blob into a code frame via HHDM + I-cache flush, maps code `RO_CODE`, stack `RW_DATA`, then `populate_stub_slot`; `mem::forget`s the `AddrSpace` (tree owned via `ttbr0_pa`).
- **`populate_stub_slot`** (441–495): sets name (`id`+`"-stub"`), `nr`, `endpoint=boot_endpoint(nr)`, `priv_id`, `priority=SRV_Q(8)`, `quantum_ms=5`, EL0 regs (`elr_el1=entry`, `sp_el0=stack_top`, `spsr_el1=0x340`), `ttbr0_pa`, `asid`, `heap_window`, and crucially `rts_flags = RTS_NO_PRIV if frozen else 0` (493–494).
- **VA layout:** code A–E = `0x40_0000, 0x41_0000, 0x42_0000, 0x43_0000, 0x44_0000`; stacks A–E = `0x80_0000..0x84_0000` (lines 54–76). `SERVER_STACK_VA=0x20_0000` shared across all servers (121, distinct per-TTBR0).
- **Priv slots:** `STUB_{A,B,C,D}_PRIV_ID = 16,17,18,19` (92–95); **E has none** (`None`, 225–235). Shared `USER_PRIV_ID=20`.
- **Stub E frozen build + release:** `build_stub(STUB_E_PROC_NR, None, b'E', …, frozen=true)` (225–235). E is **not** enqueued — only A–D are (262–266). PM releases it via `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` → `do_privctl` sets `priv_id=USER_PRIV_ID` and `rts_unset(RTS_NO_PRIV)`, which enqueues when that was the last block bit.
- **`install_stub_*_priv` helpers:** `install_one_stub_priv` (A/B, `SRV_T`) 608–621; `install_stub_c_priv` (`USR_T`, ipc_to SYSTEM, k_call_mask just `SYS_GETINFO`) 526–550; `install_stub_d_priv` (`USR_T`, ipc_to VM, opens reverse VM→D) 568–601.
- **`load_boot_server`** (295–344): ELF via `boot_image::elf::load_into`, maps stack at `SERVER_STACK_VA`, sets regs, `rts_flags=0`, enqueues. Boot servers occupy their real slots already populated by `init_boot_image`.

**Boot IMAGE ordering — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/proc/table.rs`** (`IMAGE` 113–260, `N_IMAGE=16`):
- Tasks (slots via `proc_index`): ASYNCM, IDLE, CLOCK, SYSTEM, HARDWARE (`NR_TASKS=5`).
- Servers by `nr`: **PM=0**, VFS=1, RS=2, MEM=3, TTY=4, DS=5, MFS=6, VM=7, PFS=8, SCHED=9, **INIT_PROC_NR=10** (last boot server; `NR_BOOT_PROCS=11`).
- Stubs: `STUB_A..E = 11..15` (from `kernel-shared/src/com.rs`). `USER_PRIV_ID=20` (table.rs 342), asserted `< NR_SYS_PROCS(64)`.
- `populate_user_priv` (358–390): USER priv = `USR_T` traps, `ipc_to` open to PM only, empty `k_call_mask`, `sig_mgr=PM`; opens reverse PM→USER bit. Comment at 335–342 explicitly states "the 4.6 fork path hands every forked child this same slot so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot `PRIV_TABLE`."

### A3. Trace / verification infrastructure

- **`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs`**: `CALL_COUNT` (54), `TRACE_EVERY=100` (63), `TRACE_HEAD=24` (77). Print gate `n <= TRACE_HEAD || n % TRACE_EVERY == 0`. Format (123): `[ipc {n}] caller={caller_nr} call={call_nr} target={:#x} result={result}`.
- **`[alarm N]`** (ipc/mod.rs 388): `[alarm {n}] owner={} nr={} at={now}`, HEAD=6/EVERY=100.
- **`[ksys N]` generic sampler** (system/mod.rs 133): `[ksys {n}] caller={} call={call_idx} result={result}`.
- **Named `[ksys …]` head traces (HEAD=6 each, once-per-boot events — ideal for fork/exit/wait observability):** `SYS_PRIVCTL` (do_privctl.rs 94), `SYS_EXIT` (do_exit.rs 96 `[ksys SYS_EXIT] target={} nr={} result=0`), `SYS_KILL` (do_sig.rs 99), `SYS_GETKSIG` (167), `SYS_ENDKSIG` (213), `VMCTL_PT_MAP/UNMAP` (do_vmctl.rs).
- **`[pf]`** (exception.rs 213): `[pf] proc={} far={:#x} flags={:#x} -> VM`.
- **`[noq]`** (sched.rs 399): `[noq {n}] proc={} nr={} -> scheduler={:#x}`, head-gated.
- **`[as]`** address-space summary lines (userland.rs 330, 645).

**Current SYS_FORK state:** `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:24` — `do_fork` is still an `enosys_stub!` (`ENOSYS`). Dispatch table `dispatch_caller_local` routes `SYS_FORK => stubs::do_fork` (system/mod.rs 206); target-taking calls (`SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`, `SYS_KILL`…) are routed in `kernel_call_dispatch` (176–186). `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18` is const-asserted (mod.rs 201). `SYS_FORK=KERNEL_CALL+2=0x602` (callnr.rs 16). **4.6 must implement `do_fork` and likely move it to the target-taking branch** (it operates on a child slot).

**Current PM server state** (`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/servers/pm/src/main.rs`): loop handles only `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`→`drain_ksigs` and `PM_GETPID`→`handle_getpid` (94–104). `mproc.rs` has `NR_MPROCS=32` with headroom "for slice 4.6's fork" (31–33), `MF_DEAD` kept "until 4.6's exit/wait lands zombie + reap semantics" (39–41). Module docstrings repeatedly flag fork/exec/wait as "part B (slice 4.6)."

---

## PART B — MINIX 3 reference semantics

### B4. Kernel fork — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/system/do_fork.c`

**"Copy all, then patch" (58–133):**
- Precondition: parent must be `RTS_RECEIVING` ("fork done synchronously", 51–54) and `!MF_DELIVERMSG` (48).
- `save_fpu(rpp)`; capture `gen = _ENDPOINT_G(rpc->p_endpoint)`; **`*rpc = *rpp`** (full struct copy, 63); then patch obliterated fields.
- **Endpoint/generation:** `++gen`, wrap to `1` at `_ENDPOINT_MAX_GENERATION` (69–70); `rpc->p_nr = slot` (71); `rpc->p_endpoint = _ENDPOINT(gen, p_nr)` (72).
- **Child retval:** `rpc->p_reg.retreg = 0` (74) — child's syscall returns 0 so it knows it is the child.
- Reset accounting: `p_user_time/p_sys_time=0`, `p_virt_left/p_prof_left=0`, `reset_proc_accounting`, `p_cpu_time_left/p_cycles/p_kcall_cycles/p_kipc_cycles/p_tick_cycles=0`, `cpuavg_init`.
- **Misc flags:** clears `MF_VIRT_TIMER|MF_PROF_TIMER|MF_SC_TRACE|MF_SPROF_SEEN|MF_STEP` (78–79). Note `MF_DELIVERMSG` is **carried by the struct copy**; `m_krn_lsys_sys_fork.msgaddr = rpp->p_delivermsg_vir` is returned to the caller (112).
- **RTS handling:** `RTS_SET(rpc, RTS_NO_QUANTUM)` — not runnable until scheduled (90); if `flags & PFF_VMINHIBIT` also `RTS_SET(RTS_VMINHIBIT)` (115–117); `RTS_UNSET(rpc, RTS_SIGNALED|RTS_SIG_PENDING|RTS_P_STOP)` + `sigemptyset` (122–123).
- **Privilege:** if parent `SYS_PROC`: `rpc->p_priv = priv_addr(USER_PRIV_ID)` + `RTS_SET(RTS_NO_PRIV)` (105–108) — child frozen until caller re-privileges. (This is exactly the frozen-then-`SYS_PRIVCTL` model minix.rs stub E prototypes.)
- Name gets `"*F"` appended (83–87). Clears `p_cr3`/`p_ttbr` (125–131).

**Classic tree `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/rminix/kernel/system/do_fork.c`** (simpler): same `*rpc=*rpp` then patch `p_nr`/`p_endpoint` (via `p_gen[]` array, 42–43); sets `NO_MAP` instead of `RTS_VMINHIBIT`, `retreg=0`, and **shares the quantum**: `rpc->p_ticks_left=(t+1)/2; rpp->p_ticks_left/=2` (58–59). Same `NO_PRIV` for SYS_PROC parent.

### B5. PM fork/exit/wait — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/servers/pm/forkexit.c`

- **`do_fork`** (44–140): bail if table full (`LAST_FEW=2` superuser reserve); round-robin `next_child` scan for `!IN_USE`; **`vm_fork(parent_ep, next_child, &child_ep)` first** — "PM may not fail fork after vm_fork, as VM calls sys_fork" (82); `procs_in_use++`; `*rmc = *rmp`; restore `mp_sigact` pointer + memcpy sigact array (88–89); `mp_parent=who_p`; clear tracer unless `TO_TRACEFORK`; **inherit only `(IN_USE|DELAY_CALL|TAINTED)` — `PRIV_PROC` is NOT inherited** in normal fork (106); reset child times/exitstatus/sigstatus/itimers; `mp_endpoint=child_ep`; `new_pid=get_free_pid()`; build `VFS_PM_FORK` msg (endpt/pendpt/cpid); `tell_vfs(rmc,&m)`; **`return SUSPEND`** — reply deferred until VFS replies.
- **`reply(child, 0)` pattern:** the child is woken with reply value 0 so it sees pid 0. In normal fork this happens in the VFS-reply handler (not in this file); **`do_srv_fork`** (145–240, RS-only) shows the direct form: `reply(rmc-mproc, OK)` to wake the child (237) and `return rmc->mp_pid` to the parent (RS). PM thus replies to **both** parent (pid of child) and child (0).
- **`do_exit`** (245–262): `PRIV_PROC` → `sys_kill(SIGKILL)`; else `exit_proc(mp, status, FALSE)`; `return SUSPEND` ("can't communicate from beyond the grave").
- **`exit_proc`** (267–413): cancel `ALARM_ON` timer; `sys_times` accounting into `mp_child_utime/stime`; **`sys_stop` → `PROC_STOPPED`** (prevents scheduling mid-teardown, 326–330); `vm_willexit`; panic-guard INIT/VFS; build `VFS_PM_EXIT`/`VFS_PM_DUMPCORE`, `tell_vfs`; **if `PRIV_PROC`, `sys_clear` immediately** (don't wait for VFS, 361–369); reduce `mp_flags &= (IN_USE|VFS_CALL|PRIV_PROC|TRACE_EXIT|PROC_STOPPED)` then `|= EXITING` (374–375); `mp_exitstatus=status`; if `!dump_core` → **`zombify`**; **disinherit children to `INIT_PROC_NR`** (388–409, sets `NEW_PARENT` if child mid-`VFS_CALL`, re-`check_parent` any ZOMBIE child); SIGHUP to process group if session leader.
- **`exit_restart`** (418–469, after VFS reply): `sched_stop`; `mp_scheduler=NONE`; `zombify` if not yet; if `!PRIV_PROC` `sys_clear`; `vm_exit` (release memory); wake tracer if `TRACE_EXIT`; if `TOLD_PARENT` → `cleanup`.
- **`do_wait4`** (474–564): scan children; a qualifying `ZOMBIE` child → `tell_parent(rp, addr)`, then `cleanup` if not `(VFS_CALL|EVENT_CALL)`, `return SUSPEND`; else if `children>0`: `WNOHANG`→return 0, otherwise set `WAITING`, save `mp_wpid`/`mp_waddr`, `return SUSPEND`; no children → `ECHILD`.
- **`wait_test`** (569–588): `(mp_flags & WAITING) && (pidarg==-1 || pidarg==child pid || -pidarg==child procgrp)`.
- **`zombify`** (593–624): panic if already zombie; tracer-first path (`TRACE_ZOMBIE`) else `ZOMBIE`; then `check_parent`.
- **`check_parent`** (629–665): parent `EXITING`→noop; else `wait_test`→`tell_parent`+optional `cleanup`; else **`sig_proc(parent, SIGCHLD)`**.
- **`tell_parent`** (670–726): panics on bad/not-zombie/already-told; optional `rusage` copy via `sys_datacopy`; `parent->mp_reply.status = W_EXITCODE(exitstatus, sigstatus)`; **`reply(parent, child pid)`**; clear parent `WAITING`, clear child `ZOMBIE`, set `TOLD_PARENT`; accumulate child times into parent.
- **`cleanup`** (795–806): `mp_pid=0; mp_flags=0; times=0; procs_in_use--`.
- **mproc flags** (`mproc.h` 85–104): `IN_USE 0x1, WAITING 0x2, ZOMBIE 0x4, PROC_STOPPED 0x8, ALARM_ON 0x10, EXITING 0x20, TOLD_PARENT 0x40, VFS_CALL 0x400, NEW_PARENT 0x800, PRIV_PROC 0x2000, TRACE_EXIT 0x8000, TRACE_ZOMBIE 0x10000, DELAY_CALL 0x20000, TAINTED 0x40000, EVENT_CALL 0x80000`.

### B6. Kernel exit / endpoint cleanup — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/system/do_clear.c` + `system.c`

- **`do_exit.c`** (kernel `SYS_EXIT`, 14–24): system-proc self-terminate → `cause_sig(caller, SIGABRT)`; `return EDONTREPLY`.
- **`do_clear`** (do_clear.c 17–78, PM-only): `release_address_space`; skip if `isemptyp`; remove IRQ hooks; **`clear_endpoint(rc)`**; `reset_kernel_timer(alarm)`; `RTS_SETFLAGS(rc, RTS_SLOT_FREE)`; `release_fpu`; if `SYS_PROC` → `priv(rc)->s_proc_nr = NONE`. **Teardown order: address space → IRQ hooks → IPC (`clear_endpoint`) → alarm timer → slot free → priv release.**
- **`clear_endpoint`** (system.c 539–571): `RTS_SET(RTS_NO_ENDPOINT)`; zero `s_asynsize` if SYS_PROC; `clear_ipc(rc)`; `clear_ipc_refs(rc, EDEADSRCDST)`; `clear_memreq(rc)`.
- **`clear_ipc`** (508–534): if `RTS_SENDING`, walk the **destination's `p_caller_q`** and splice `rc` out (only the one queue it can be on), `RTS_UNSET(SENDING)`; always `RTS_UNSET(RECEIVING)`.
- **`clear_ipc_refs`** (576–606): cancel pending async sends targeting `rc`; for every proc `rp`: unset `notify_pending`/`asyn_pending` bits for `rc`'s priv; **if `P_BLOCKEDON(rp) == rc->p_endpoint` → `rp->p_reg.retreg = EDEADSRCDST` and `clear_ipc(rp)`** — this is how procs sending-to or receiving-from the dead endpoint are unblocked with `EDEADSRCDST`.
- Init generation: proc.c:133 `p_endpoint = _ENDPOINT(0, p_nr)` (gen 0); fork bumps it. `EDEADSRCDST` also enforced at send/receive time (proc.c 897–899, 999–1001, 1282–1283).
- minix.rs parallel: `do_exit.rs` docstring (17–21) explicitly defers to 4.6 "unblocking receivers blocked RECEIVE-from-target with `EDEADSRCDST`, address-space teardown via `AddrSpace::destroy`, freeing the slot (`RTS_SLOT_FREE`), and the endpoint generation bump." Its `unlink_from_caller_q` (108–139) already implements the `clear_ipc` send-queue splice. **4.6 must add the `clear_ipc_refs` EDEADSRCDST unblock loop and slot/generation reuse.**

### B7. VM fork — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/servers/vm/fork.c` (32–115)

`vm_isokendpt`/slot checks; `origpt = vmc->vm_pt` preserved; **`*vmc = *vmp`** (copy parent vmproc); `vmc->vm_slot=childproc`; **`region_init(&vmc->vm_regions_avl)`** (fresh region tree); `vmc->vm_endpoint=NONE`; restore `vmc->vm_pt=origpt`; `pt_new` (fresh page table); **`map_proc_copy(vmc, vmp)`** clones parent regions into child; inherit only `VMF_INUSE`; `acl_fork`; **`sys_fork(parent_ep, childproc, &child_ep, PFF_VMINHIBIT, &msgaddr)`** — VM is the caller that drives the kernel `do_fork` (hence `PFF_VMINHIBIT` so the child isn't scheduled until its pagetable is bound); `pt_bind`; `handle_memory_once` makes both parent+child message pages writable; returns `msg->VMF_CHILD_ENDPOINT`.

**Conceptual takeaway for minix.rs:** MINIX VM deep-clones the parent's whole region AVL + a new pagetable. minix.rs VM is a much simpler **static region table** (`servers/vm/` region tracking, per the userland.rs/CLAUDE notes), so 4.6's VM-fork analog would clone a per-proc region-table slot rather than an AVL tree — and the ordering constraint (VM calls kernel `sys_fork` with a VM-inhibit flag, binds the child pagetable, then the child becomes schedulable) is the piece to preserve.

---

### Key implementation touchpoints for the 4.6 plan
- Kernel: replace `enosys_stub!(do_fork)` in `kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:24` with a real `do_fork` (copy-all-then-patch: new endpoint+generation, `retreg=0`, `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`+`RTS_NO_PRIV`, `USER_PRIV_ID`); route it in the target-taking branch of `kernel_call_dispatch` (system/mod.rs 176–186); bump `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` const-assert only if adding calls; extend `do_exit.rs` with the `clear_ipc_refs`/`EDEADSRCDST` unblock + slot free + generation bump.
- Endpoint/generation: `Proc` currently gen-0 only (`boot_endpoint`); 4.6 introduces the generation bump — see `endpoint::{endpoint_proc, make_endpoint}` usage.
- PM server (`servers/pm/src/main.rs` + `mproc.rs`): add `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` handlers (new `PM_RQ_BASE+n` constants in `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`, widen `NR_PM_MSGS`); `mproc.rs` already has `NR_MPROCS=32` headroom, `MF_DEAD`, and parent-slot tracking to build zombie/reap on.
- New EL0 exerciser stub (or extend E) in `user_stub.S` following E's PM-SENDREC shape, wired through `userland.rs` `build_stub`.
- Verification: fork/exit/wait are once-per-boot, so head-based `[ksys …]` traces (HEAD=6) plus a new PM-side or `[ksys SYS_FORK]` head trace mirroring `do_exit.rs`/`do_privctl.rs` will make the flow observable in the QEMU serial log.

(Note: I did not create the plan file — as a read-only exploration agent I'm returning findings directly for the parent agent to fold into the plan.)

content.text:
I have gathered a comprehensive picture across both codebases. Here is my report.

---

# Slice 4.6 preparation — exploration findings

## PART A — minix.rs userland glue

### A1. EL0 user stubs — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S`

**Shared conventions (all stubs):**
- Constant block (lines 40–55): `SEND=1 RECEIVE=2 SENDREC=3 ANY=0x3FFF`, endpoints `ENDPOINT_A=11 ENDPOINT_B=12 ENDPOINT_PM=0 ENDPOINT_VM=7 ENDPOINT_SYS=0x7FFE`, `PM_GETPID=0x700`, `VM_BRK=0xC01 VM_MMAP=0xC02 VM_MUNMAP=0xC03`, `MSG_SIZE=104`.
- **Message on stack:** `sub x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE` carves a 104-byte `Message` at `[SP_EL0-104, SP_EL0)`; `str wzr,[x9,#0]` zeroes `m_source` (kernel overwrites), `str w..,[x9,#4]` sets `m_type`, payload begins at `[x9,#8]`. SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 % 16 handled; stack pages are 4 KiB).
- **SVC ABI:** `x0`=src/dst endpoint, `x1`=primitive, `x2`=msg ptr, then `svc #0`. Result returns in `x0`.
- **Callee-saved counters:** `x19..x23` (AAPCS) persist across SVC because `trap.S` saves/restores `x0..x30`. This is load-bearing for stub D's `x22` (mmap base survives an intervening SVC) and every stub's `x19` counter.
- **PC-relative-free:** all blobs use only immediate `mov/movz/movk` + register-relative loads, so they run correctly copied to any code VA.

**Per-stub catalog:**
- **Stub A** (63–78) sender: `x19` counter, `SENDREC` to `ENDPOINT_B` in a loop; `m_type`=counter.
- **Stub B** (86–105) echo server: `RECEIVE(ANY)`, reads `m_source` (`sxtw` sign-extend), `SEND` back, mirrors `m_type`.
- **Stub C** (113–134) kernel-call client: `SENDREC` to `ENDPOINT_SYS` with `m_type=SYS_GETINFO`, `payload[0..4]=GET_WHOAMI`.
- **Stub D** (176–263) VM driver: `VM_BRK`→touch→`VM_BRK`→touch→`VM_MMAP`(base returned in `x22`)→touch→`VM_MUNMAP`→32-iter heap steady phase→**deliberate out-of-region store to `x22`** to trigger SIGSEGV (the slice-4.5 kill chain).
- **Stub E** (281–299) — **the 4.6 template**, PM getpid client. Exact sequence:
  ```
  mov  x19, #0                 // counter
  1:  sub  x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE   // 104-byte msg buffer
      str  wzr, [x9, #0]       // m_source = 0 (kernel overwrites)
      mov  w10, #PM_GETPID     // 0x700
      str  w10, [x9, #4]       // m_type; no request payload
      mov  x0, #ENDPOINT_PM    // 0
      mov  x1, #SENDREC        // 3
      mov  x2, x9
      svc  #0
      add  x19, x19, #1
      b    1b
  ```
  E never reads the reply body (reply `m_type`=E's pid, `payload[0..4]`=parent pid). **For 4.6**: a fork/wait/exit exerciser would follow this exact PM-SENDREC shape but with new `m_type` values `PM_RQ_BASE(0x700)+n`. Today only `PM_GETPID` exists (see A-notes on `callnr.rs`); `NR_PM_MSGS` locks the PM message count and would need widening.

### A2. Bootstrap wiring — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`

- **`build_stub`** signature (359–369): `(nr, priv_id: Option<PrivId>, id, stub_start, stub_end, code_va, stack_va, heap_window, frozen: bool)`. Allocates a fresh `AddrSpace`, copies the blob into a code frame via HHDM + I-cache flush, maps code `RO_CODE`, stack `RW_DATA`, then `populate_stub_slot`; `mem::forget`s the `AddrSpace` (tree owned via `ttbr0_pa`).
- **`populate_stub_slot`** (441–495): sets name (`id`+`"-stub"`), `nr`, `endpoint=boot_endpoint(nr)`, `priv_id`, `priority=SRV_Q(8)`, `quantum_ms=5`, EL0 regs (`elr_el1=entry`, `sp_el0=stack_top`, `spsr_el1=0x340`), `ttbr0_pa`, `asid`, `heap_window`, and crucially `rts_flags = RTS_NO_PRIV if frozen else 0` (493–494).
- **VA layout:** code A–E = `0x40_0000, 0x41_0000, 0x42_0000, 0x43_0000, 0x44_0000`; stacks A–E = `0x80_0000..0x84_0000` (lines 54–76). `SERVER_STACK_VA=0x20_0000` shared across all servers (121, distinct per-TTBR0).
- **Priv slots:** `STUB_{A,B,C,D}_PRIV_ID = 16,17,18,19` (92–95); **E has none** (`None`, 225–235). Shared `USER_PRIV_ID=20`.
- **Stub E frozen build + release:** `build_stub(STUB_E_PROC_NR, None, b'E', …, frozen=true)` (225–235). E is **not** enqueued — only A–D are (262–266). PM releases it via `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` → `do_privctl` sets `priv_id=USER_PRIV_ID` and `rts_unset(RTS_NO_PRIV)`, which enqueues when that was the last block bit.
- **`install_stub_*_priv` helpers:** `install_one_stub_priv` (A/B, `SRV_T`) 608–621; `install_stub_c_priv` (`USR_T`, ipc_to SYSTEM, k_call_mask just `SYS_GETINFO`) 526–550; `install_stub_d_priv` (`USR_T`, ipc_to VM, opens reverse VM→D) 568–601.
- **`load_boot_server`** (295–344): ELF via `boot_image::elf::load_into`, maps stack at `SERVER_STACK_VA`, sets regs, `rts_flags=0`, enqueues. Boot servers occupy their real slots already populated by `init_boot_image`.

**Boot IMAGE ordering — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/proc/table.rs`** (`IMAGE` 113–260, `N_IMAGE=16`):
- Tasks (slots via `proc_index`): ASYNCM, IDLE, CLOCK, SYSTEM, HARDWARE (`NR_TASKS=5`).
- Servers by `nr`: **PM=0**, VFS=1, RS=2, MEM=3, TTY=4, DS=5, MFS=6, VM=7, PFS=8, SCHED=9, **INIT_PROC_NR=10** (last boot server; `NR_BOOT_PROCS=11`).
- Stubs: `STUB_A..E = 11..15` (from `kernel-shared/src/com.rs`). `USER_PRIV_ID=20` (table.rs 342), asserted `< NR_SYS_PROCS(64)`.
- `populate_user_priv` (358–390): USER priv = `USR_T` traps, `ipc_to` open to PM only, empty `k_call_mask`, `sig_mgr=PM`; opens reverse PM→USER bit. Comment at 335–342 explicitly states "the 4.6 fork path hands every forked child this same slot so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot `PRIV_TABLE`."

### A3. Trace / verification infrastructure

- **`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs`**: `CALL_COUNT` (54), `TRACE_EVERY=100` (63), `TRACE_HEAD=24` (77). Print gate `n <= TRACE_HEAD || n % TRACE_EVERY == 0`. Format (123): `[ipc {n}] caller={caller_nr} call={call_nr} target={:#x} result={result}`.
- **`[alarm N]`** (ipc/mod.rs 388): `[alarm {n}] owner={} nr={} at={now}`, HEAD=6/EVERY=100.
- **`[ksys N]` generic sampler** (system/mod.rs 133): `[ksys {n}] caller={} call={call_idx} result={result}`.
- **Named `[ksys …]` head traces (HEAD=6 each, once-per-boot events — ideal for fork/exit/wait observability):** `SYS_PRIVCTL` (do_privctl.rs 94), `SYS_EXIT` (do_exit.rs 96 `[ksys SYS_EXIT] target={} nr={} result=0`), `SYS_KILL` (do_sig.rs 99), `SYS_GETKSIG` (167), `SYS_ENDKSIG` (213), `VMCTL_PT_MAP/UNMAP` (do_vmctl.rs).
- **`[pf]`** (exception.rs 213): `[pf] proc={} far={:#x} flags={:#x} -> VM`.
- **`[noq]`** (sched.rs 399): `[noq {n}] proc={} nr={} -> scheduler={:#x}`, head-gated.
- **`[as]`** address-space summary lines (userland.rs 330, 645).

**Current SYS_FORK state:** `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:24` — `do_fork` is still an `enosys_stub!` (`ENOSYS`). Dispatch table `dispatch_caller_local` routes `SYS_FORK => stubs::do_fork` (system/mod.rs 206); target-taking calls (`SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`, `SYS_KILL`…) are routed in `kernel_call_dispatch` (176–186). `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18` is const-asserted (mod.rs 201). `SYS_FORK=KERNEL_CALL+2=0x602` (callnr.rs 16). **4.6 must implement `do_fork` and likely move it to the target-taking branch** (it operates on a child slot).

**Current PM server state** (`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/servers/pm/src/main.rs`): loop handles only `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`→`drain_ksigs` and `PM_GETPID`→`handle_getpid` (94–104). `mproc.rs` has `NR_MPROCS=32` with headroom "for slice 4.6's fork" (31–33), `MF_DEAD` kept "until 4.6's exit/wait lands zombie + reap semantics" (39–41). Module docstrings repeatedly flag fork/exec/wait as "part B (slice 4.6)."

---

## PART B — MINIX 3 reference semantics

### B4. Kernel fork — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/system/do_fork.c`

**"Copy all, then patch" (58–133):**
- Precondition: parent must be `RTS_RECEIVING` ("fork done synchronously", 51–54) and `!MF_DELIVERMSG` (48).
- `save_fpu(rpp)`; capture `gen = _ENDPOINT_G(rpc->p_endpoint)`; **`*rpc = *rpp`** (full struct copy, 63); then patch obliterated fields.
- **Endpoint/generation:** `++gen`, wrap to `1` at `_ENDPOINT_MAX_GENERATION` (69–70); `rpc->p_nr = slot` (71); `rpc->p_endpoint = _ENDPOINT(gen, p_nr)` (72).
- **Child retval:** `rpc->p_reg.retreg = 0` (74) — child's syscall returns 0 so it knows it is the child.
- Reset accounting: `p_user_time/p_sys_time=0`, `p_virt_left/p_prof_left=0`, `reset_proc_accounting`, `p_cpu_time_left/p_cycles/p_kcall_cycles/p_kipc_cycles/p_tick_cycles=0`, `cpuavg_init`.
- **Misc flags:** clears `MF_VIRT_TIMER|MF_PROF_TIMER|MF_SC_TRACE|MF_SPROF_SEEN|MF_STEP` (78–79). Note `MF_DELIVERMSG` is **carried by the struct copy**; `m_krn_lsys_sys_fork.msgaddr = rpp->p_delivermsg_vir` is returned to the caller (112).
- **RTS handling:** `RTS_SET(rpc, RTS_NO_QUANTUM)` — not runnable until scheduled (90); if `flags & PFF_VMINHIBIT` also `RTS_SET(RTS_VMINHIBIT)` (115–117); `RTS_UNSET(rpc, RTS_SIGNALED|RTS_SIG_PENDING|RTS_P_STOP)` + `sigemptyset` (122–123).
- **Privilege:** if parent `SYS_PROC`: `rpc->p_priv = priv_addr(USER_PRIV_ID)` + `RTS_SET(RTS_NO_PRIV)` (105–108) — child frozen until caller re-privileges. (This is exactly the frozen-then-`SYS_PRIVCTL` model minix.rs stub E prototypes.)
- Name gets `"*F"` appended (83–87). Clears `p_cr3`/`p_ttbr` (125–131).

**Classic tree `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/rminix/kernel/system/do_fork.c`** (simpler): same `*rpc=*rpp` then patch `p_nr`/`p_endpoint` (via `p_gen[]` array, 42–43); sets `NO_MAP` instead of `RTS_VMINHIBIT`, `retreg=0`, and **shares the quantum**: `rpc->p_ticks_left=(t+1)/2; rpp->p_ticks_left/=2` (58–59). Same `NO_PRIV` for SYS_PROC parent.

### B5. PM fork/exit/wait — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/servers/pm/forkexit.c`

- **`do_fork`** (44–140): bail if table full (`LAST_FEW=2` superuser reserve); round-robin `next_child` scan for `!IN_USE`; **`vm_fork(parent_ep, next_child, &child_ep)` first** — "PM may not fail fork after vm_fork, as VM calls sys_fork" (82); `procs_in_use++`; `*rmc = *rmp`; restore `mp_sigact` pointer + memcpy sigact array (88–89); `mp_parent=who_p`; clear tracer unless `TO_TRACEFORK`; **inherit only `(IN_USE|DELAY_CALL|TAINTED)` — `PRIV_PROC` is NOT inherited** in normal fork (106); reset child times/exitstatus/sigstatus/itimers; `mp_endpoint=child_ep`; `new_pid=get_free_pid()`; build `VFS_PM_FORK` msg (endpt/pendpt/cpid); `tell_vfs(rmc,&m)`; **`return SUSPEND`** — reply deferred until VFS replies.
- **`reply(child, 0)` pattern:** the child is woken with reply value 0 so it sees pid 0. In normal fork this happens in the VFS-reply handler (not in this file); **`do_srv_fork`** (145–240, RS-only) shows the direct form: `reply(rmc-mproc, OK)` to wake the child (237) and `return rmc->mp_pid` to the parent (RS). PM thus replies to **both** parent (pid of child) and child (0).
- **`do_exit`** (245–262): `PRIV_PROC` → `sys_kill(SIGKILL)`; else `exit_proc(mp, status, FALSE)`; `return SUSPEND` ("can't communicate from beyond the grave").
- **`exit_proc`** (267–413): cancel `ALARM_ON` timer; `sys_times` accounting into `mp_child_utime/stime`; **`sys_stop` → `PROC_STOPPED`** (prevents scheduling mid-teardown, 326–330); `vm_willexit`; panic-guard INIT/VFS; build `VFS_PM_EXIT`/`VFS_PM_DUMPCORE`, `tell_vfs`; **if `PRIV_PROC`, `sys_clear` immediately** (don't wait for VFS, 361–369); reduce `mp_flags &= (IN_USE|VFS_CALL|PRIV_PROC|TRACE_EXIT|PROC_STOPPED)` then `|= EXITING` (374–375); `mp_exitstatus=status`; if `!dump_core` → **`zombify`**; **disinherit children to `INIT_PROC_NR`** (388–409, sets `NEW_PARENT` if child mid-`VFS_CALL`, re-`check_parent` any ZOMBIE child); SIGHUP to process group if session leader.
- **`exit_restart`** (418–469, after VFS reply): `sched_stop`; `mp_scheduler=NONE`; `zombify` if not yet; if `!PRIV_PROC` `sys_clear`; `vm_exit` (release memory); wake tracer if `TRACE_EXIT`; if `TOLD_PARENT` → `cleanup`.
- **`do_wait4`** (474–564): scan children; a qualifying `ZOMBIE` child → `tell_parent(rp, addr)`, then `cleanup` if not `(VFS_CALL|EVENT_CALL)`, `return SUSPEND`; else if `children>0`: `WNOHANG`→return 0, otherwise set `WAITING`, save `mp_wpid`/`mp_waddr`, `return SUSPEND`; no children → `ECHILD`.
- **`wait_test`** (569–588): `(mp_flags & WAITING) && (pidarg==-1 || pidarg==child pid || -pidarg==child procgrp)`.
- **`zombify`** (593–624): panic if already zombie; tracer-first path (`TRACE_ZOMBIE`) else `ZOMBIE`; then `check_parent`.
- **`check_parent`** (629–665): parent `EXITING`→noop; else `wait_test`→`tell_parent`+optional `cleanup`; else **`sig_proc(parent, SIGCHLD)`**.
- **`tell_parent`** (670–726): panics on bad/not-zombie/already-told; optional `rusage` copy via `sys_datacopy`; `parent->mp_reply.status = W_EXITCODE(exitstatus, sigstatus)`; **`reply(parent, child pid)`**; clear parent `WAITING`, clear child `ZOMBIE`, set `TOLD_PARENT`; accumulate child times into parent.
- **`cleanup`** (795–806): `mp_pid=0; mp_flags=0; times=0; procs_in_use--`.
- **mproc flags** (`mproc.h` 85–104): `IN_USE 0x1, WAITING 0x2, ZOMBIE 0x4, PROC_STOPPED 0x8, ALARM_ON 0x10, EXITING 0x20, TOLD_PARENT 0x40, VFS_CALL 0x400, NEW_PARENT 0x800, PRIV_PROC 0x2000, TRACE_EXIT 0x8000, TRACE_ZOMBIE 0x10000, DELAY_CALL 0x20000, TAINTED 0x40000, EVENT_CALL 0x80000`.

### B6. Kernel exit / endpoint cleanup — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/system/do_clear.c` + `system.c`

- **`do_exit.c`** (kernel `SYS_EXIT`, 14–24): system-proc self-terminate → `cause_sig(caller, SIGABRT)`; `return EDONTREPLY`.
- **`do_clear`** (do_clear.c 17–78, PM-only): `release_address_space`; skip if `isemptyp`; remove IRQ hooks; **`clear_endpoint(rc)`**; `reset_kernel_timer(alarm)`; `RTS_SETFLAGS(rc, RTS_SLOT_FREE)`; `release_fpu`; if `SYS_PROC` → `priv(rc)->s_proc_nr = NONE`. **Teardown order: address space → IRQ hooks → IPC (`clear_endpoint`) → alarm timer → slot free → priv release.**
- **`clear_endpoint`** (system.c 539–571): `RTS_SET(RTS_NO_ENDPOINT)`; zero `s_asynsize` if SYS_PROC; `clear_ipc(rc)`; `clear_ipc_refs(rc, EDEADSRCDST)`; `clear_memreq(rc)`.
- **`clear_ipc`** (508–534): if `RTS_SENDING`, walk the **destination's `p_caller_q`** and splice `rc` out (only the one queue it can be on), `RTS_UNSET(SENDING)`; always `RTS_UNSET(RECEIVING)`.
- **`clear_ipc_refs`** (576–606): cancel pending async sends targeting `rc`; for every proc `rp`: unset `notify_pending`/`asyn_pending` bits for `rc`'s priv; **if `P_BLOCKEDON(rp) == rc->p_endpoint` → `rp->p_reg.retreg = EDEADSRCDST` and `clear_ipc(rp)`** — this is how procs sending-to or receiving-from the dead endpoint are unblocked with `EDEADSRCDST`.
- Init generation: proc.c:133 `p_endpoint = _ENDPOINT(0, p_nr)` (gen 0); fork bumps it. `EDEADSRCDST` also enforced at send/receive time (proc.c 897–899, 999–1001, 1282–1283).
- minix.rs parallel: `do_exit.rs` docstring (17–21) explicitly defers to 4.6 "unblocking receivers blocked RECEIVE-from-target with `EDEADSRCDST`, address-space teardown via `AddrSpace::destroy`, freeing the slot (`RTS_SLOT_FREE`), and the endpoint generation bump." Its `unlink_from_caller_q` (108–139) already implements the `clear_ipc` send-queue splice. **4.6 must add the `clear_ipc_refs` EDEADSRCDST unblock loop and slot/generation reuse.**

### B7. VM fork — `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/servers/vm/fork.c` (32–115)

`vm_isokendpt`/slot checks; `origpt = vmc->vm_pt` preserved; **`*vmc = *vmp`** (copy parent vmproc); `vmc->vm_slot=childproc`; **`region_init(&vmc->vm_regions_avl)`** (fresh region tree); `vmc->vm_endpoint=NONE`; restore `vmc->vm_pt=origpt`; `pt_new` (fresh page table); **`map_proc_copy(vmc, vmp)`** clones parent regions into child; inherit only `VMF_INUSE`; `acl_fork`; **`sys_fork(parent_ep, childproc, &child_ep, PFF_VMINHIBIT, &msgaddr)`** — VM is the caller that drives the kernel `do_fork` (hence `PFF_VMINHIBIT` so the child isn't scheduled until its pagetable is bound); `pt_bind`; `handle_memory_once` makes both parent+child message pages writable; returns `msg->VMF_CHILD_ENDPOINT`.

**Conceptual takeaway for minix.rs:** MINIX VM deep-clones the parent's whole region AVL + a new pagetable. minix.rs VM is a much simpler **static region table** (`servers/vm/` region tracking, per the userland.rs/CLAUDE notes), so 4.6's VM-fork analog would clone a per-proc region-table slot rather than an AVL tree — and the ordering constraint (VM calls kernel `sys_fork` with a VM-inhibit flag, binds the child pagetable, then the child becomes schedulable) is the piece to preserve.

---

### Key implementation touchpoints for the 4.6 plan
- Kernel: replace `enosys_stub!(do_fork)` in `kernel/src/system/stubs.rs:24` with a real `do_fork` (copy-all-then-patch: new endpoint+generation, `retreg=0`, `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`+`RTS_NO_PRIV`, `USER_PRIV_ID`); route it in the target-taking branch of `kernel_call_dispatch` (system/mod.rs 176–186); bump `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` const-assert only if adding calls; extend `do_exit.rs` with the `clear_ipc_refs`/`EDEADSRCDST` unblock + slot free + generation bump.
- Endpoint/generation: `Proc` currently gen-0 only (`boot_endpoint`); 4.6 introduces the generation bump — see `endpoint::{endpoint_proc, make_endpoint}` usage.
- PM server (`servers/pm/src/main.rs` + `mproc.rs`): add `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` handlers (new `PM_RQ_BASE+n` constants in `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`, widen `NR_PM_MSGS`); `mproc.rs` already has `NR_MPROCS=32` headroom, `MF_DEAD`, and parent-slot tracking to build zombie/reap on.
- New EL0 exerciser stub (or extend E) in `user_stub.S` following E's PM-SENDREC shape, wired through `userland.rs` `build_stub`.
- Verification: fork/exit/wait are once-per-boot, so head-based `[ksys …]` traces (HEAD=6) plus a new PM-side or `[ksys SYS_FORK]` head trace mirroring `do_exit.rs`/`do_privctl.rs` will make the flow observable in the QEMU serial log.
